propositions: No CI workflow enables pip dependency caching, so every job re-downloads and rebuilds the heavy dependency stack.
Add cache: 'pip' and cache-dependency-path: pyproject.toml to every actions/setup-python step that installs project deps across all workflows.
The cache is keyed on pyproject.toml content so a dependency bump automatically invalidates the cache; there is no separate lockfile.
Caching is purely additive: a cache miss behaves exactly as today and affected install steps remain otherwise unchanged.
Enabling caching roughly halves install time by avoiding re-downloads, saving multiplied across the ~10+ jobs that install deps.
